followUps.text: How do you think that one change might have rippled through your life?
Have you been able to talk to your parents about it?

prompts.text: What would you say to the version of you who was trying to survive your family as a kid?

followUps.text: What did that younger version of you need that they weren't getting?
What do you still carry from the way they learned to cope?

prompts.text: What's a routine your family had that shaped you more than anyone realizes?

followUps.text: Was it something intentional or just the way things were?
How does it still show up in your life now?

prompts.text: What role do you play in your family, and how did that happen?

followUps.text: Is it a role you want, or one that was assigned to you?
Who might you be if you didn't have to carry that role?

prompts.text: What conversation are you dreading that you know needs to happen?

followUps.text: What's the worst outcome you're imagining?
What would waiting any longer cost you?

prompts.text: What would change if everyone in this family said what they actually meant?

followUps.text: Would it bring you closer or blow things apart?
Who has the most they're holding back?

prompts.text: What's a family secret that shaped you more than anyone realizes?

followUps.text: How did you find out about it?
What did carrying that knowledge do to you?

prompts.text: What's the biggest lie your family tells itself?

followUps.text: Who benefits from keeping that lie alive?
What do you think the truth would ask of everyone?

prompts.text: What's something from your childhood in this family that still catches up with you?

followUps.text: When does it usually come back to the surface?
What do you think it still wants from you now?

prompts.text: What's a wound from your family that still hasn't healed?

followUps.text: What keeps it from healing?
What would healing actually look like for you?

prompts.text: When did you stop trying to earn a family member's approval?

followUps.text: What was the final straw?
How did letting go of it change you?

prompts.text: What's something you resent about how responsibilities are divided in your family?

followUps.text: Have you ever brought it up?
What would fair actually look like to you?

prompts.text: What's a part of your family history that feels heavy to carry?

followUps.text: Do you feel responsible for that weight?
Who else in the family carries it with you?

prompts.text: What's something a family member did that you've never really been able to forgive?

followUps.text: What do you think forgiveness would require from you?
What keeps the wound open?

prompts.text: What do you wish your parents really knew about your life right now?

followUps.text: What's stopped you from telling them?
How do you think they'd react?

prompts.text: What's a way your family made you feel small without realizing it?

followUps.text: Does it still happen?
How has that feeling shaped the way you see yourself?

prompts.text: What's a harmful pattern in your family that you're trying not to repeat?

followUps.text: When do you catch yourself falling into it?
What are you trying to practice instead?

prompts.text: What's the hardest thing about loving someone in your family?

followUps.text: What makes the love complicated?
How do you make sense of that difficulty now?

prompts.text: What's a part of your identity that your family still doesn't fully accept?

followUps.text: How does their lack of acceptance affect you day to day?
Have you stopped trying to get them to see it?

prompts.text: What would your younger self think about your relationship with your family now?

followUps.text: Would they be relieved, confused, or heartbroken?
What would you want to explain to them?

prompts.text: What's a boundary with family that you had to fight to set?

followUps.text: What did it cost you to hold that line?
Has it been respected since?

prompts.text: What's something you've never confronted a family member about but may need to?

followUps.text: What's held you back?
What would you say if you finally did?

prompts.text: What's a way your family dealt with conflict that you've had to unlearn?

followUps.text: What did you replace it with?
Was the unlearning gradual or forced by a specific moment?

prompts.text: What's the most painful thing a family member has ever said to you?

followUps.text: Do you think they knew how much it hurt?
How did it change the way you relate to them?

prompts.text: When have you felt like the black sheep of your family?

followUps.text: Was it something you did or just who you are?
Have you made peace with that outsider feeling?

prompts.text: What's something about your parents' relationship that affected how you see love?

followUps.text: Was it something you wanted to replicate or avoid?
How does it show up in your own relationships?

prompts.text: What's an unfair expectation that was placed on you growing up?

followUps.text: Who put it there?
Have you been able to let it go, or does it still drive you?

prompts.text: What's a truth about your family that nobody outside would believe?

followUps.text: What would someone need to see to understand?
How do you hold that truth and the public version at the same time?

prompts.text: What do you carry from your family that you wish you could put down?

followUps.text: What keeps you holding onto it?
What would your life look like without that weight?

prompts.text: What's a sacrifice you made for your family that went unnoticed?

followUps.text: How did it feel to give that without recognition?
Would you do it again?

prompts.text: What's something you swore you'd never do as a parent that you catch yourself doing?

followUps.text: How does it feel when you notice it?
What are you trying to do differently in spite of it?

prompts.text: When did you first realize you were repeating a family pattern?

followUps.text: What tipped you off?
Have you been able to interrupt it?

prompts.text: What's the thing your family is most in denial about?

followUps.text: What would acknowledging it require from everyone?
Do you think anyone else in the family sees it too?

prompts.text: What's a family gathering tradition that feels more like a performance than a real connection?

followUps.text: Who is the performance really for?
What would it look like if everyone just dropped the act?

prompts.text: What subject could split your family wide open if someone finally named it plainly?

followUps.text: Who has the most to lose if it gets said out loud?
Do you think the fallout would be temporary, or would it change the family for good?

prompts.text: Who in your family gets protected from the truth the most?

followUps.text: How does everyone help keep that protection in place?
What does it cost the rest of the family to keep doing that?

prompts.text: What's something you're afraid your kids — or future kids — might inherit from your family through you?

followUps.text: When do you feel that inheritance most strongly in yourself?
What are you doing, if anything, to keep it from passing through you unchanged?

prompts.text: What's a question you've always wanted to ask your parents but never have?

followUps.text: What are you afraid the answer might be?
What would it change if you finally asked?

prompts.text: What household role were you assigned growing up that you never actually agreed to?

followUps.text: How did it shape the way you show up in your family now?
Have you ever tried to set it down or hand it back?

prompts.text: What's something your family took from you that still shapes the way you live?

followUps.text: How did losing it change who you became in that family?
Do you think you can reclaim any part of it now?

prompts.text: How did losing someone in your family affect you then, and how does it affect you now?

followUps.text: What part of that loss still shows up in you today?
Has the way it lives in you changed over time, or just gone quieter?

prompts.text: What trait of your parents are you most afraid of seeing in yourself?

followUps.text: When do you catch glimpses of it?
What do you do when you see it surface?

prompts.text: How was death explained in your family when you were growing up, and does that explanation still comfort you now?

followUps.text: Did that explanation actually comfort you then, or did you just learn to live inside it?
What do you believe about death now that feels different from what you were taught?

prompts.text: What's something you've never told your parents?

followUps.text: What would it mean for them to finally know?
What's kept you from saying it all this time?

prompts.text: What's a conversation with a family member you still ache to have one more time?

followUps.text: What would you finally say if you had that chance?
What do you still wish they had said back?

prompts.text: What's a practical tension in your family that everyone works around instead of really addressing?

followUps.text: What would it take to actually address it?
Who benefits most from leaving it unresolved?

prompts.text: What do you spend too much time doing that you probably shouldn't?

followUps.text: What does it give you that keeps you coming back?
What would you do with that time if you reclaimed it?

prompts.text: What are you feeling genuinely proud of right now?

followUps.text: What did it take to get there?
Have you let yourself fully enjoy it?

prompts.text: What keeps showing up in your dreams lately?

followUps.text: Do you think it's trying to tell you something?
How does it leave you feeling when you wake up?

prompts.text: What have you been quietly getting better at without anyone noticing?

followUps.text: What's been driving that quiet improvement?
Does it matter to you whether people notice?

prompts.text: What do you do when you're completely alone and nobody can see?

followUps.text: What does that behavior say about who you really are?
Is it something you enjoy or just a habit?

prompts.text: What do you catch yourself complaining about the most?

followUps.text: What's underneath the complaint?
Is it something you can change, or do you just need to vent?

prompts.text: What's a skill you used to have that you miss more than you expected?

followUps.text: What did it let you feel or express when it was still part of your life?
Do you want it back, or did it have its time and you've moved on?

prompts.text: What's a small win you had recently that you didn't celebrate enough?

followUps.text: Why did you let it pass without acknowledgment?
What would celebrating it have felt like?

prompts.text: What's the last thing that genuinely surprised you about yourself?

followUps.text: Was it a good surprise or an unsettling one?
What do you think it reveals about where you're headed?

prompts.text: What's a comfort food that always makes everything a little better?

followUps.text: What's the memory or feeling attached to it?
Is it the taste or the ritual that comforts you?

prompts.text: What's a place that always makes you feel calm?

followUps.text: What is it about that place that settles you?
When was the last time you went there?

prompts.text: What's something you're looking forward to right now?

followUps.text: What about it excites you the most?
How does having something to look forward to change your mood?

prompts.text: What would you do with an entire day with zero obligations?

followUps.text: Would you plan it or let it unfold?
What does your ideal version of that day say about what you need?

prompts.text: What's something you've been meaning to get back into?

followUps.text: What pulled you away from it in the first place?
What would it give you to pick it up again?

prompts.text: What's the best compliment you've ever received?

followUps.text: Why did that one land so deeply?
Do you believe it about yourself?

prompts.text: What's a simple pleasure that never gets old for you?

followUps.text: What makes it feel fresh every time?
When did you first discover how much it means to you?

prompts.text: What's something you're grateful for that you rarely think about?

followUps.text: What would your life look like without it?
Why do you think it's so easy to overlook?

prompts.text: What's a skill you picked up that changed your daily life?

followUps.text: How did you come to learn it?
What difference does it make on a regular day?

prompts.text: What's your go-to way to recharge when you're running on empty?

followUps.text: Does it actually restore you, or just distract you?
How do you know when you've hit empty?

prompts.text: What's the last thing you learned that genuinely excited you?

followUps.text: What about it sparked that excitement?
Have you done anything with it since?

prompts.text: What's a childhood memory that always makes you smile?

followUps.text: What about it still feels so warm?
Who's in that memory with you?

prompts.text: What's something about your morning routine that sets the tone for your day?

followUps.text: What happens to your day when you skip it?
How did it become a ritual for you?

prompts.text: What's a personal rule you live by that most people don't know about?

followUps.text: Where did that rule come from?
Has it ever been tested?

prompts.text: What's something you've gotten more patient about as you've gotten older?

followUps.text: What taught you that patience?
Is there something you're still impatient about?

prompts.text: What's a song that always puts you in a good mood?

followUps.text: What memory or feeling does it tap into?
When do you reach for it most?

prompts.text: What's the last thing you did that felt truly spontaneous?

followUps.text: What pushed you to just go for it?
How did it feel compared to your usual planned approach?

prompts.text: What's a quality about yourself that you've come to appreciate?

followUps.text: Did you always see it as a strength, or did that take time?
How does that quality show up in your daily life?

prompts.text: What's something you collect — physically or mentally?

followUps.text: What draws you to collecting it?
What does the collection mean to you?

prompts.text: What's a place you've been that left a lasting impression on you?

followUps.text: What made it hit so hard?
Do you want to go back, or is the memory enough?

prompts.text: What's something you do purely for the joy of it, with no goal attached?

followUps.text: How did you find that thing?
What does it give you that goal-driven things don't?

prompts.text: Who have you been carrying unsaid words for lately?

followUps.text: What's making it hard to bring up?
How do you think you'd feel once you finally said it?

prompts.text: What's a small disagreement you had recently that stuck with you longer than it should have?

followUps.text: What do you think it was really about underneath?
Did you resolve it or just let it fade?

prompts.text: What do you hope people remember about you?

followUps.text: Are you living in a way that builds toward that?
What would you need to change to get closer to it?

prompts.text: When was the last time you felt completely and totally free?

followUps.text: What created that feeling?
How often do you give yourself permission to feel that way?

prompts.text: What's something you lost that you still think about?

followUps.text: What did that loss teach you about what matters?
Have you found anything that fills that space?

prompts.text: When was the last time you felt like everything was going your way?

followUps.text: What was different about that stretch?
What do you think made it possible?

prompts.text: What's something you used to care deeply about that barely matters to you now?

followUps.text: What caused the shift?
Do you see letting go of it as growth or loss?

prompts.text: What's something you've started to see more realistically over time?

followUps.text: What helped you begin to see it differently?
Do you feel better or worse having that clearer view?

prompts.text: What does a good life actually look like to you — not what anyone told you it should be?

followUps.text: How close are you to living that version?
What's the biggest gap between where you are and where you want to be?

prompts.text: What's a belief that's shaped who you are more than anything?

followUps.text: Where did that belief take root?
Has it ever been challenged?

prompts.text: What's something you've built — a habit, a relationship, a mindset — that you're really proud of?

followUps.text: What did it take to build it?
What does it give you that you didn't have before?

prompts.text: What's a part of your life that feels exactly right?

followUps.text: What makes it feel so aligned?
How did you get it to that place?

prompts.text: When you catch your reflection, what's the first thought that tends to show up?

followUps.text: Is that thought kind or critical?
Has the voice in your head always said that?

prompts.text: What's a bad habit you've tried to break but keep coming back to?

followUps.text: What does the habit give you that makes it so sticky?
What would breaking it actually require from you?

prompts.text: What kind of stress changes you in ways you might not fully see?

followUps.text: What starts to shift in you when that stress builds?
What usually helps you notice it sooner?

prompts.text: What do you find surprisingly hard to say yes to?

followUps.text: What's underneath that hesitation?
What are you protecting by holding back?

prompts.text: Where does your life feel most out of sync with who you thought you'd be by now?

followUps.text: Does that gap feel motivating or discouraging?
What would closing it require?

prompts.text: What's something you keep putting off that's actually important to you?

followUps.text: What's the real reason you keep delaying?
What would it feel like to finally do it?

prompts.text: What emotion do you have the hardest time sitting with?

followUps.text: What do you usually do to escape it?
What do you think it's trying to tell you?

prompts.text: What's a standard you hold yourself to that might be too high?

followUps.text: Where did that standard come from?
What would ease up in your life if you lowered it?

prompts.text: What's something you've been telling yourself that might not be true?

followUps.text: How long have you been repeating that story?
What would change if you let it go?

prompts.text: What's a relationship in your life that's asking for more attention?

followUps.text: What's been getting in the way?
What would showing up for it a little more actually look like?

prompts.text: What's something you're carrying that you haven't shared with anyone?

followUps.text: What would it take for you to share it?
How is carrying it alone affecting you?

prompts.text: What do you think people assume about you that's wrong?

followUps.text: What creates that false impression?
Does it bother you enough to correct it?

prompts.text: What's a decision you made recently that you're still second-guessing?

followUps.text: What's the version of events where you made the right call?
What would put the second-guessing to rest?

prompts.text: When life gets heavy, what do you need that you often fail to give yourself?

followUps.text: Why do you think you miss that need when you're under pressure?
What would make it easier to offer it to yourself?

prompts.text: What's an area of your life where you've been coasting?

followUps.text: What would it look like if you started trying again?
What let you start coasting in the first place?

prompts.text: How do you know when stress is starting to harden you a little?

followUps.text: What tends to harden first — your tone, your patience, or something else?
What helps you soften again?

prompts.text: What would change in your life if you stopped trying to please everyone?

followUps.text: Who would you disappoint first?
What would you gain by being honest instead?

prompts.text: What's something you used to be sure about that you're not sure about anymore?

followUps.text: What made the certainty crack?
Is the uncertainty uncomfortable or freeing?

prompts.text: What do you think your biggest distraction in life is right now?

followUps.text: What is it distracting you from?
What would you have to face without it?

prompts.text: What's a part of your personality you've been hiding lately?

followUps.text: What made you decide to tuck it away?
Do you miss that part of yourself?

prompts.text: What's a compliment you struggle to accept about yourself?

followUps.text: Why is it so hard to let in?
What would it mean if you believed it?

prompts.text: What's something you need to forgive yourself for?

followUps.text: What's been standing in the way of that forgiveness?
What would letting it go free you to do?

prompts.text: When was the last time you were truly honest with yourself about something uncomfortable?

followUps.text: What did you admit?
How did it feel once you stopped avoiding it?

prompts.text: What's a boundary you need to set but haven't?

followUps.text: What's been holding you back from drawing that line?
What would change once it's in place?

prompts.text: What's the gap between how you present yourself and how you actually feel?

followUps.text: Who in your life sees closest to the real version?
What would it take to close that gap?

prompts.text: What motivates you more — fear of failure or desire for something better?

followUps.text: How does that motivator shape the way you take risks?
Which one do you wish drove you more?

prompts.text: What part of getting older has made you feel more tender toward yourself?

followUps.text: What do you think that tenderness is teaching you?
Where do you still resist offering it to yourself?

prompts.text: What's a way you've grown in the last year that you haven't acknowledged?

followUps.text: Why is it so hard to give yourself credit?
What does that growth mean for who you're becoming?

prompts.text: What's something you keep avoiding because it would mean real change?

followUps.text: What's the change you're most afraid of?
What would life look like on the other side of it?

prompts.text: What part of getting older has made you more guarded?

followUps.text: What are you trying to protect when that guard goes up?
What helps you feel safe enough to soften?

prompts.text: What's something you're slowly becoming unable to unsee about yourself?

followUps.text: What helped you start to notice it?
How has recognizing it changed anything in your life?

prompts.text: What's a decision you keep avoiding even though you know it matters?

followUps.text: What makes the avoidance feel safer than deciding?
What's the cost of continuing to delay?

prompts.text: How does it feel when you're the one holding the power in a situation?

followUps.text: Are you comfortable with power, or does it make you uneasy?
How do you handle the responsibility that comes with it?

prompts.text: When do you feel yourself responding from stress instead of from your actual feelings?

followUps.text: What usually gets lost in you when stress takes over?
What would help you respond more truthfully in those moments?

prompts.text: What fear about aging feels harder to carry out loud than in your head?

followUps.text: What makes that fear easier to hold silently than to say out loud?
What would change if you let yourself name it more honestly?

prompts.text: When are you most proud of having stood up for yourself?

followUps.text: What gave you the courage to do it?
How did it change what you expect from yourself?

prompts.text: What have you been carrying longer than it still deserves space in you?

followUps.text: What would happen if you finally let it go?
What has holding onto it been doing for you?

prompts.text: When was the last time you bent the rules in a way that still sits with you?

followUps.text: Do you feel guilty about it or justified?
Would you do it again?

prompts.text: What kind of people make you feel the most envious, and why?

followUps.text: What does the envy reveal about what you want?
Is it something you could actually pursue?

prompts.text: How has time made you stronger, and how has it made you more fragile?

followUps.text: Where do you feel your strength most clearly now?
Where do you feel your fragility most sharply?

prompts.text: What's a boundary you've set recently that felt like real progress?

followUps.text: What made you finally draw the line?
How has life been different since?

prompts.text: When did you start to feel that perfect justice might not exist?

followUps.text: What experience made that idea feel less real?
How has that changed the way you see fairness or people?

prompts.text: What's something you once believed would always work out, but now you're not so sure about?

followUps.text: What caused that belief to shift?
How do you feel holding that uncertainty now?

prompts.text: What do you think you will need more and more as you age?

followUps.text: What kind of care or steadiness do you think will matter most to you?
What part of you still struggles to admit that need?

prompts.text: When do you feel most aware that your time is not unlimited?

followUps.text: What does that awareness make you want to protect or change?
How does it affect the way you want to live right now?

prompts.text: What do you know you need to let go of but can't seem to release?

followUps.text: What would your life look like without it?
What keeps your grip so tight?

prompts.text: What's the gap between who you are and who you want to be — and what lives in that gap?

followUps.text: What fills that space right now — fear, habit, or something else?
What's the first step toward closing that gap?

prompts.text: What do you have a complicated relationship with that's hard to explain?

followUps.text: What makes it so hard to put into words?
Does the complication bother you, or have you accepted it?

prompts.text: When are you most tempted to break your own rules?

followUps.text: What does giving in usually cost you?
Is the temptation about the thing itself or the rebellion?

prompts.text: What's a feeling you've been swallowing instead of expressing?

followUps.text: What are you afraid would happen if you let it out?
How is holding it in affecting you?

prompts.text: When negative thoughts about yourself start to take hold, how do you usually respond?

followUps.text: Do you know how to comfort yourself in those moments?
What kind of response from yourself actually helps?

prompts.text: What's a part of your past you'd erase if you could?

followUps.text: What would be different about you without that experience?
Is there any part of it that made you who you are in a way you value?

prompts.text: What's a way you self-sabotage that you're fully aware of?

followUps.text: What do you think you're trying to avoid by doing it?
What would it take to stop the cycle?

prompts.text: What's a truth about your life that you dress up when you talk about it?

followUps.text: What's the undressed version?
Why does the real version feel too vulnerable to share?

prompts.text: What's something you pretend to have figured out but really haven't?

followUps.text: What would it mean to admit you're still lost on it?
Who would you go to for help if you could?

prompts.text: What's a coping mechanism you rely on that isn't actually healthy?

followUps.text: What does it numb or avoid?
What might a healthier replacement give you instead?

prompts.text: What's something your inner life knows about you that your outer life still won't admit?

followUps.text: What keeps that truth from making it into the open?
How is that split between what you know and how you live affecting you?

prompts.text: What's the most painful thing you've realized about yourself?

followUps.text: How did that realization land?
What have you done with that knowledge?

prompts.text: What's a desire you have that you feel ashamed of?

followUps.text: Where does the shame come from — you or someone else's voice?
What would it feel like to want it without the guilt?

prompts.text: What's a version of yourself you've buried that sometimes still surfaces?

followUps.text: When does it tend to come back?
Is it a version you miss or one you're trying to outgrow?

prompts.text: What's one of the hardest parts of being you that nobody really sees?

followUps.text: What would it mean for someone to finally see it?
How do you carry that alone?

prompts.text: What's a promise you've broken to yourself more than once?

followUps.text: What gets in the way every time?
What would it take to finally keep it?

prompts.text: What's a relationship you stayed in too long and why?

followUps.text: What kept you there past the expiration?
What did leaving — or not leaving — teach you?

prompts.text: What's something you're afraid to want because you might not get it?

followUps.text: What would it cost you to want it fully?
What's worse — not getting it, or never trying?

prompts.text: What do you think your life would look like if you stopped performing for others?

followUps.text: Who would you lose?
Who would you finally attract?

prompts.text: What's a memory that still makes you feel shame when it surfaces?

followUps.text: What exactly triggers the shame?
Have you been able to talk to anyone about it?

prompts.text: What's the most honest thing you could say about where you are in life right now?

followUps.text: How does that honesty feel?
What would you change first if you could?

prompts.text: What's something you need that you've convinced yourself you don't?

followUps.text: When did you start telling yourself you didn't need it?
What would admitting the need change for you?

prompts.text: What keeps you up at night that you've never told anyone?

followUps.text: What makes it feel too heavy to share?
What would you need from someone to feel safe telling them?

prompts.text: What's a pattern in your relationships that you can't seem to break?

followUps.text: When did you first notice it repeating?
What do you think it's trying to resolve?

prompts.text: What's the thing you're most afraid of other people finding out about you?

followUps.text: What feels most at risk if other people knew?
What do you imagine would actually happen if it came to light?

prompts.text: What's something you've numbed yourself to that you know you need to eventually face?

followUps.text: How are you avoiding it?
What do you think would happen if you addressed it?

prompts.text: What's a part of your story you've rewritten to make it easier to tell?

followUps.text: What did you leave out and why?
What would the unedited version reveal about you?

prompts.text: What's one thing about the way you're living right now that you know isn't really working anymore?

followUps.text: Why have you kept trying to make it work anyway?
What would changing it force you to face?

prompts.text: What's something you keep calling selflessness that may actually be self-erasure?

followUps.text: What does it let you avoid having to ask for directly?
What has it cost you to keep disappearing that way?

prompts.text: Do you like yourself, or do you mostly like yourself under certain conditions?

followUps.text: What are those conditions?
How steady is that feeling, really?

prompts.text: Is there a hurt in you that never seems to fully heal?

followUps.text: Is this something you can tend on your own, or do you need help from someone else?
What do you think healing would actually require?

prompts.text: What's a habit you keep returning to that you know isn't good for you?

followUps.text: What does it give you in the moment that's hard to get elsewhere?
What would your life actually look like if you finally stopped?

prompts.text: What's something you've wanted to say to someone for a long time but know you probably never will?

followUps.text: What would saying it actually cost you?
What does keeping it inside cost you instead?

prompts.text: What's something you wish you'd never had to see?

followUps.text: How did seeing it change you?
Have you been able to process it?

prompts.text: Where in your life do you quietly fear that if people looked closer, they'd find less than you present?

followUps.text: What are you working so hard to make sure they don't see?
How much of that fear feels earned, and how much of it feels old?

prompts.text: How would you honestly describe your relationship with your body?

followUps.text: When is that relationship at its hardest?
What would a healthier version of it look like?

prompts.text: What's a truth about yourself you've been circling without fully letting it land?

followUps.text: What would have to change if you let it become fully real?
What keeps you hovering around it instead of stepping into it?

prompts.text: What have you been faking lately that you're tired of keeping up?

followUps.text: Who are you performing for?
What would dropping the act actually cost you?

prompts.text: What's a lie you told once that you still think about?

followUps.text: Why does it still have a hold on you?
If you wanted to make it right now, what might that look like?

prompts.text: When did you first really realize you would die one day?

followUps.text: How old were you, and what made that realization land?
Does that awareness change how you think about your life now?

prompts.text: How much of your daily life is shaped around avoiding what you don't want to feel?

followUps.text: What feelings are you most skilled at dodging?
What would a day without that avoidance actually look like?

prompts.text: What's something you've been putting off being honest with yourself about?

followUps.text: What would the honest version sound like if you said it out loud?
What feels most likely to shift once you admit it?

prompts.text: Is there a relationship in your life that you know has run its course?

followUps.text: What's keeping you from walking away?
What would you grieve if you let it go?

prompts.text: What's a part of yourself that you know you need to outgrow?

followUps.text: What's that part been protecting you from?
Who would you become without it?

prompts.text: What's something you still carry guilt about?

followUps.text: What would it take to finally put it down?
What makes it so hard to offer yourself any forgiveness?

prompts.text: What's a belief you hold deeply but have never actually said out loud?

followUps.text: What makes it feel too risky to share?
What would it mean to own it publicly?

prompts.text: What's something that hurt you that you still minimize when you tell the story?

followUps.text: What feels safer about making it sound smaller than it was?
What would the fuller version reveal about what it actually did to you?

prompts.text: If you could erase one memory completely, which would you choose?

followUps.text: What would life feel like without it?
Is there anything that memory gave you that you'd lose too?

prompts.text: What's the worst thing you've done to someone that still weighs on you?

followUps.text: Have you tried to make it right?
What would you say to them now?

prompts.text: When have you felt the most humiliated in your life?

followUps.text: How did that experience shape the way you protect yourself?
Have you healed from it, or does it still sting?

prompts.text: If you got to choose how your life story ends, what would it look like?

followUps.text: What would the last chapter be about?
Are you living in a way that moves toward that ending?

prompts.text: How did being treated badly by someone shape the way you see yourself?

followUps.text: Do you still carry their version of you?
What would it take to replace it with your own?

prompts.text: What's something that happened that permanently changed who you are?

followUps.text: Was the change something you chose or something that happened to you?
Do you recognize yourself on both sides of it?

prompts.text: When was a time you felt rejected that really stung?

followUps.text: What did the rejection make you believe about yourself?
Have you been able to separate the rejection from your worth?

prompts.text: Where are you still waiting for a kind of closure that may never come?

followUps.text: What keeps you waiting for it instead of grieving it?
What would it mean to stop making your peace dependent on that ending?

prompts.text: What principle would you never compromise on, no matter what?

followUps.text: When has that principle been tested the hardest?
What does it protect in you that nothing else can?

prompts.text: What part of your daily routine is actually a coping mechanism you've never examined?

followUps.text: When did it start, and what were you going through at the time?
What would you have to face if you took it away?
